reasoning: The LLM fidelity analysis identified 2 major defects involving missing double asterisks (which denote probable action items) and flattened list hierarchies. Programmatic analysis found 2 major structural defects related to a table split across pages and missing `<th>` headers. With a total of 4 major defects, the document falls into the HUMAN_REVIEW tier according to the count-based routing rules.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: The source document uses a double asterisk (**) at the end of several agenda items to denote them as 'Probable Action Items' (as defined in the footer). In the output, these double asterisks have been deleted from multiple items, including the 'Approval of Agenda' and 'Approval of Meeting Minutes' at 9:10 a.m., the 'Vote on Notice of Text...' at 2:30 p.m., and the first two 'Vote on approval...' bullets at 3:00 p.m. This omission changes the meaning by removing the indicator that these items are
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
Approval of Agenda **
Approval of Meeting Minutes**
concerns.output_quote:
Approval of Agenda 
Approval of Meeting Minutes

concerns.analysis: On page 2, there is an unordered list for 'Informational Materials continued'. In the source document, 'Observer Program' and 'Incidental Take Permit Updates' are indented as sub-items (children) under 'Protected Resources Update'. Similarly, 'Red Drum' and 'Southern Flounder' are sub-items under 'Landings Update'. In the JSON output, these sub-items have been flattened and placed at the same level as their parent items within the 'children' array.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
− Protected Resources Update
 Observer Program
 Incidental Take Permit Updates
− Landings Update
 Red Drum
 Southern Flounder
concerns.output_quote: {"text": "Protected Resources Update"}, {"text": "Observer Program"}, {"text": "Incidental Take Permit Updates"}, {"text": "Landings Update"}, {"text": "Red Drum"}, {"text": "Southern Flounder"}
concerns.location: Page 2, second element (list under 'Informational Materials continued:')

concerns.description: Table split across page break(s): 1 table(s) with matching structure across consecutive pages
concerns.analysis: split_table_count=1. Examples: pages 1-2: 2 cols (9:00 a.m., meeting overview – lara klibansky...). Tables spanning multiple pages were extracted as separate table elements. In HTML, these should be one continuous table to maintain accessibility and reading order.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=0, no_th_tables=1. Tables use <th> for data values, have a data row mismarked as a header row, or lack <th> elements entirely. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (3): Marine Fisheries Commission Business Meeting Agenda; 1; 2
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=3. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Footnote(s) converted to bulleted list: 1 unordered list(s) after table(s) appear to be footnotes
concerns.analysis: footnote_as_list_count=1. Examples: 1 item(s): "Times indicated are merely for guidance. The commi". Table footnotes (often marked with *, †, ‡) were converted to <ul><li> elements instead of paragraphs. This changes the semantic meaning — screen readers announce them as list items rather than explanatory notes.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have time values in <th> cells: "9:00 a.m."
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=1, no_th_tables=1. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.

action_items: Restore the double asterisks (**) to the relevant agenda items.
Correct the list hierarchy under 'Protected Resources Update' and 'Landings Update' so the sub-items are properly nested.
Merge the table split across the page break into a single continuous table.
Ensure the table has proper `<th>` markup for accessibility.
Remove leftover header/footer text from the HTML output.
